text: The laparoscopic camera is inserted through the umbilical port, with three additional trocars placed below the chest towards the right side for instrument access.
The surgeon elevates the artery, creating a tunnel-like space beneath it to access the yellow bile duct emerging from the area.
The surgeon places clips on the blood vessels supplying the gallbladder, lifting the tissue to clean around the area. The yellow tissue covering the vessels is visible as the dissection progresses.
The surgeon prepares to cut and separate the cystic duct and artery, ensuring clean blood flow to the channel while managing any bleeding.
The surgeon carefully separates the gallbladder from the liver bed, detaching it from its resting position within the liver's anatomical structure.
The surgeon separates the gallbladder from the liver bed, ensuring the area remains dry to prevent contamination. The procedure involves creating four small openings under the right occipital spine.

The surgeon creates four incisions in the chest area: two incisions measuring one centimeter each and two incisions measuring half a centimeter each, with a slightly larger opening at the bottom of the chest.
